turns.content: right so I need halal restaurant recs in the Northern Quarter in Manchester. like actually good ones not just the ones that come up first on google

Sure thing! The Northern Quarter has a solid halal scene. A few worth checking out:

- **Yadgar** on Thomas Street — Pakistani food, proper portions, really flavourful curries. One of the more established spots.
- **Kabana** on Hilton Street — another Pakistani one, been around for years, no-frills but the food is consistently great. Cash only last time anyone checked.
- **This & That** on Soap Street — rice and three concept, loads of choices, very reasonably priced. Popular at lunchtime.
- **Bakchich** — Lebanese, slightly more upscale vibe, great mezze platters.

What kind of cuisine are you in the mood for? That'll help narrow it down.

ooh I've been to Kabana bare times loool the food is sick but yeah it's cash only which is so annoying. I was thinking more like nice enough to take someone out? like a friend's birthday not a canteen vibe

Ah gotcha — birthday-worthy, nice atmosphere, not just good food but somewhere that feels like an occasion. That changes things:

- **Dishoom** on Portland Street — technically just outside the NQ but walkable. Indian, gorgeous interiors, great cocktail menu (non-alcoholic options too). Really popular so you'd want to book.
- **Elnecot** on Cutting Room Square — it's in Ancoats which borders the NQ. Modern British but they do halal options, nice atmosphere, great for a group.
- **Rudy's** — Neapolitan pizza, there's one on Peter Street and one in Ancoats. Not officially certified halal but no pork on the menu and vegetarian/seafood options are safe.
- **Mowgli** — Indian street food, trendy interiors, good for groups.

How many people are you thinking, and does the whole group eat halal or just you?

it's like 6 of us and yeah most of us eat halal so it needs to be fully halal not just halal options on the side. Dishoom is nice tbh but we went there for someone else's birthday like 2 months ago so can't really repeat

Fair enough, you don't want to be the group that only goes to one place! For fully halal and birthday-worthy for 6:

- **Zouk Tea Bar & Grill** on Chester Street — Pakistani/Indian, really nice interior, proper restaurant feel, not too pricey. They do great lamb chops and their chai is class. Good for groups.
- **MyLahore** on Stockport Road — it's a bit further out (Longsight) but the food is incredible, big portions, fun atmosphere. Very popular with groups.
- **Evuna** on Thomas Street — Spanish tapas, NQ location. They have a good halal selection and the sharing plates work well for birthdays.
- **Bem Brasil** — Brazilian steakhouse on King Street, they have halal meat options and the rodizio experience is genuinely fun for a birthday.

Zouk is probably your safest bet — fully halal, nice ambiance, central enough, and handles group bookings well.
Zouk is a shout actually I've heard about it but never been. do they take bookings for a Saturday night or is it walk in only?

Zouk definitely takes bookings and for a Saturday night with 6 people I'd strongly recommend booking ahead — they get busy, especially weekends. You can usually book through their website or give them a call. I'd try to book at least a week in advance for a Saturday. They're pretty good with group setups too, so if you mention it's a birthday they might sort out a nice table arrangement.

perfect. the birthday is in like 3 weeks so I've got time. do you know roughly what the price range is per person? trying to work out if we split the bill or do that thing where everyone covers the birthday girl

Zouk is mid-range — you're probably looking at around £20-30 per person for food, more if people go for the mixed grill platters or the lamb chops (which are worth it). Drinks (soft drinks, mocktails, chai) will add maybe £5-8 per person. So roughly £25-35 per person all in.

For 6 people covering the birthday girl, that means 5 people splitting her share too — so maybe an extra £5-7 each on top of your own. Very doable. The classic move is to quietly sort out the bill split before the food arrives so there's no awkward calculator moment at the end.
loool the awkward calculator moment is TOO real. okay I'm going with Zouk. gonna book it tonight. thanks for this you've actually been proper helpful
Happy to help! Zouk is a great choice — your friend's going to love it. Enjoy the birthday dinner, and yeah, book tonight before the good time slots go. Have a good one!
wait one more thing — do they do like dessert or cake situations? or should we bring our own cake and ask them to bring it out? I've seen people do that at restaurants but idk if it's a thing at Zouk

Zouk does have a dessert menu — they do gulab jamun, kheer, and a few other bits. But for a birthday you'd probably want an actual cake. A lot of restaurants in Manchester are fine with you bringing your own cake as long as you give them a heads up. I'd call Zouk when you book and just ask — most places will plate it up for you and some even do sparklers if you ask nicely. Alternatively you could do dessert somewhere else after — there's a few good dessert spots nearby like Archie's or Ice Shack.
oh Archie's after would be so good actually the milkshakes there are unreal. might do dinner at Zouk then walk to Archie's for dessert. that's a proper birthday plan init

That's a solid plan — Zouk for the main event, then Archie's for the sweet finish. They're not far from each other so the walk in between is nice too, especially if the weather's decent. Your friend is lucky to have someone putting this much thought into it. Have a great night when it happens!
